items.title: 15 stocks in focus as govt approves Rs 8.7 lakh crore Jal Jeevan Mission extension. Check full list
items.description: As many as 15 stocks may be in focus after the Union Cabinet approved an extension of the Jal Jeevan Mission till 2028 with a total outlay of Rs 8.69 lakh crore. The decision is expected to benefit EPC firms, pump makers and pipe companies, reviving payments and boosting order visibility across the water infrastructure ecosystem.

items.title: IndiGo shares in focus as CEO Pieter Elbers resigns just 3 months after December flight disruptions
items.description: IndiGo shares will be in focus after CEO Pieter Elbers resigned nearly three months after major operational disruptions that stranded passengers. Managing Director Rahul Bhatia will oversee operations temporarily. The airline also faces sector risks from rising crude prices and geopolitical tensions, though brokerages still see long-term growth potential.

items.title: Stocks to buy in 2026 for long term: Ambuja Cement, Apollo Hospitals among 5 stocks that could give 10-30% return
items.description: Brokerage firms continue to highlight selective opportunities across sectors such as cement, healthcare, banking, and consumer electronics, as companies benefit from improving demand trends and strong long-term growth drivers.We have collated a list of recommendations from top brokerage firms from ETNow and other sources:

items.title: Nifty's 18-month slump: How the AI shift and wars are rewriting the bull playbook
items.description: Indian equity investors face an 18-month return drought, with the Nifty and smallcaps significantly below recent peaks. Despite fears surrounding AI and geopolitical tensions, strategists urge against raising cash, advocating for increased equity allocation due to attractive valuations and a potential market turn.

items.title: Reliance Industries shares in focus as Trump announces $300 billion US refinery project with company backing
items.description: Reliance Industries shares will be in focus after U.S. President Donald Trump announced a proposed $300 billion oil refinery project in Texas with investment backing from the Indian conglomerate. The facility at the Port of Brownsville is expected to boost U.S. energy output and exports, though Reliance has not confirmed the partnership.

items.title: Sedemac Mechatronics shares to list today. Here's what GMP indicates
items.description: The Rs 1,087 crore IPO of Sedemac Mechatronics had opened for subscription from March 4 to March 6. The public issue was entirely an offer for sale (OFS) of about 80.43 lakh shares, meaning the company itself will not receive any proceeds from the issue and the funds will go to existing shareholders selling their stakes.

items.title: Positive Breakout: These 12 stocks cross above their 200 DMAs
items.description: (Daily Moving Averages) on March 10, 2026, according to stockedge.com's technical scan data. The 200-day daily moving average (DMA) is used by traders as a key indicator for determining the overall trend in a particular stock. As long as the stock is priced above the 200-day SMA on the daily timeframe, it is generally considered to be in an overall uptrend. Take a look:

items.title: GIFT Nifty down 25 points; here's the trading setup for today's session
items.description: Indian benchmark indices concluded March 10 on a strong note, with the Nifty surpassing 24,250. Analysts anticipate consolidation between 24,500 and 23,700, with immediate support at 24,150. The India VIX saw a significant drop, while foreign investors were net sellers.

items.title: Stocks in news: Nazara Technologies, IndiGo, Hindustan Zinc, NTPC Green, RIL
items.description: Indian markets saw a strong rebound on Tuesday, recovering some losses. Global cues improved and crude oil price worries eased. Several companies are in focus today. Nazara Technologies saw a significant block deal. IndiGo's CEO stepped down. RIL is boosting LPG production. Vedanta calls for unlocking domestic resources. NTPC Green Energy added new solar capacity.

items.title: AIFs could get upto Rs 17,000 crore after PFRDA opens NPS door
items.description: India's pension fund regulator has permitted 1% of the ₹17 lakh crore assets under management to be invested in alternative investment funds. This move aims to channel domestic institutional money into private equity and venture capital, addressing a long-standing gap in the alternatives ecosystem.

items.title: D-St bulls, rupee regain ground amid global oil price rollercoaster
items.description: Indian stocks and the rupee saw a strong rebound on Tuesday.  This recovery followed a significant drop in crude oil prices.  Asian markets also experienced gains.  Traders cut bearish bets as tensions eased.  Investors are deploying cash into the market.  Sectoral indices mostly climbed, with auto and consumer durables leading the gains.  Volatility remains a concern for the market.

items.title: RBI aligns capital rules of banks with global norms
items.description: The Reserve Bank of India has updated bank capital rules, aligning them with global standards and clarifying how lenders assess trading partner default risk. New guidelines require banks to include trading-partner exposures from all consolidated entities.  Updated 'add-on' percentages for off-balance-sheet trades like derivatives are now in effect, impacting capital requirements for clearing members on exchanges.

items.title: RBI links bank dividends to Common Equity Tier 1 ratio
items.description: The Reserve Bank of India has revised dividend payout norms for banks, linking them to Common Equity Tier 1 (CET1) ratios instead of CRAR and NPAs. The new guidelines, effective April 1, 2027, offer greater flexibility to well-capitalized banks, with payouts ranging from 0% to 100% of adjusted profit after tax, subject to a cap.

items.title: These 9 banking stocks have an upside potential of more than 16% in 1 year, according to analysts
items.description: The way things are panning out in the Gulf war, the probability of ever greater volatility in markets across the globe is high. Given that the Indian economy and oil have a strong correlation which can impact many sectors, it might be better to think twice before acting in the stock market. And remember this: Don't take any statement by any of the parties involved as gospel truth. Check out Stock Reports Plus, powered by Refinitiv, for price targets of over 4,000 listed stocks along with detailed company analysis focusing on five key components ,earnings, fundamentals, relative valuation, risk, and price momentum, to generate standardised scores. SR+ Reports is a complimentary offering to ETPrime members.

items.title: Aluminium stocks jump up to 10% as global supply disruptions lift prices
items.description: Global aluminium prices have surged due to plant shutdowns in Qatar and Bahrain and Strait of Hormuz disruptions, boosting Indian producers like Nalco, Hindalco, and Vedanta.  These supply issues are expected to widen the global aluminium deficit, potentially driving prices higher through the first half of FY27.

items.title: Valuations moderate after market fall, but India’s premium limits FII comeback
items.description: Indian stock markets have seen valuations ease after a recent sell-off. However, key indices still trade at a premium compared to emerging market peers. This valuation gap, coupled with investor caution, is impacting foreign fund inflows. India's equity market performance has been among the weakest globally this year. Investors remain watchful of geopolitical events.

items.title: Cupid shares plunge 6% after 15% rally over two days: What lies ahead?
items.description: Cupid shares experienced a dip on Wednesday  after a two-day rally that drove the stock 15% higher after the company's 4:1 bonus issue.  In January this year, Cupid announced its board of directors had approved the issue of bonus shares in the ratio of 4:1. The company had set March 9 as the record date to determine the eligibility of shareholders to receive the bonus shares.

items.title: Reports of my death are greatly exaggerated! Why Nuvama is screaming buy on all top 10 IT stocks
items.description: Nuvama says fears that Gen-AI will cripple India’s IT services model are overblown, invoking Mark Twain’s line that “reports of my death are greatly exaggerated.” After a sharp AI-driven selloff that pushed valuations to multi-year lows, the brokerage has upgraded all top 10 IT stocks to “BUY,” arguing the sector is poised for a comeback as AI services demand accelerates.

items.title: Happiest Minds among 5 stocks showing bullish RSI upswing
items.description: On March 10, five stocks from the Nifty500 universe that gained over 10% were highlighted in the RSI Trending Up scan, based on data from StockEdge.com. It is an uptrend signal if the RSI crosses above 50 from below. "RSI trending up" means the Relative Strength Index (RSI) is increasing, indicating strengthening momentum in the stock's price. This can indicate potential upward movement and may be used by traders to identify buying opportunities.

items.title: Don't miss this train: BofA upgrades Sona Comstar on 3 growth engines
items.description: Sona Comstar shares surged over 4% after BofA Securities upgraded the stock to 'buy' with a Rs 640 target price. The brokerage anticipates strong growth driven by its traction motors, driveline business recovery, and an underappreciated railways segment, projecting a 24% upside.

items.title: Happiest Minds Tech shares zoom 35% in just 3 days! What’s behind the stellar surge?
items.description: Happiest Minds Technologies rose sharply on Wednesday, extending gains to over 35% in three days, driven by a sharp upgrade in its FY27 growth outlook to 12.5% and strong traction from its AI First strategy. Management cited broad-based demand, improved pipelines and accelerating AI-led adoption across sectors as key factors behind the stock’s sharp rally.

items.title: Adani Total Gas, GAIL & other gas stocks rally up to 15% amid supply worries in India. Here's why
items.description: Gas stocks rallied sharply on Wednesday as investors bet the Iran–Israel conflict may ease, potentially relieving supply constraints. The rebound follows last week’s sharp fall triggered by escalating tensions. India continues to face a deepening gas crisis, with prices surging and the government prioritising PNG, CNG and LPG to ensure uninterrupted supply.

items.title: Buy, Sell or Hold: Motilal Oswal initiates coverage on Jio Financial Services; recommends buy on Bikaji Foods
items.description: Motilal Oswal Financial Services remains bullish on select stocks across consumer, metals and financial services sectors. The brokerage maintained a Buy rating on Bikaji Foods and JSW Steel and initiated coverage on Jio Financial Services with a Buy call, citing strong growth visibility, capacity expansion and long term structural opportunities.

items.title: Jefferies raises Coal India target price, says valuation reasonable
items.description: Jefferies has raised Coal India target to Rs 485, citing reasonable valuation and improving earnings. The brokerage forecasts 9% CAGR in FY26–28, supported by higher e-auction premiums, rising dispatch volumes, and a rebound in power demand. Coal India remains a cash-rich, high-dividend PSU stock with strong market share.

items.title: Trading is a serious pursuit: Why India needs to treat it like a profession
items.description: When people think about investing in the stock market, they often associate it with two distinct horizons: long term wealth creation and short term profits.

items.title: IT stocks in focus after Oracle’s strong results; Nuvama says valuations now attractive after correction
items.description: Indian IT stocks may remain in focus after Oracle reported stronger than expected quarterly results and raised its revenue outlook. Nuvama said the recent 20% correction in Indian IT stocks amid concerns over AI disruption has made valuations attractive, though near term volatility may continue as the industry adapts to Gen AI.

items.title: Nazara Tech shares jump 4% as Morgan Stanley acquires stake worth Rs 69 crore via block deal
items.description: Nazara Technologies shares surged 4% after Morgan Stanley Asia Singapore Pte acquired shares worth Rs 69.2 crore in a block deal. The gaming company, India's only listed one, reported strong Q3FY26 operational performance with revenue of Rs 406 crore and EBITDA rising 29% year-on-year.

items.title: Sensex slumps 900 points, Nifty tests 24,000: Top 4 factors behind today's market crash
items.description: Indian stock markets saw a steep fall on Wednesday. The Sensex dropped over 900 points and the Nifty50 neared 24,000. This decline followed a flat opening amid global uncertainty. Key factors included ongoing Iran-Israel conflict, a weakening Rupee, continued FII selling, and profit booking after recent gains. Reliance Industries shares rose on news of a US refinery partnership.

items.title: ​Authum Investment among 5 stocks flashing bullish signals, hinting at a possible uptrend
items.description: On March 10, five stocks in the Nifty500 pack that gained over 6% appeared on the bullish scanner of "White Marubozu", according to StockEdge.com’s technical scan data. A White Marubozu is a bullish candlestick pattern in technical analysis. It occurs when the opening price is the lowest for a given period and the closing price is the highest.Represented by a long white (or green) candlestick with no upper shadow and a small or non-existent lower shadow, it indicates strong buying pressure throughout the session. Traders interpret it as a signal that buyers were in control from the opening to the closing, often using such patterns for informed decisions about future price movements in financial markets.
